Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Debra Lynn Pulliam, To Submit To Mental Examination, Supporting Declaration Of Christine H. Long And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Mar-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CONCORD AUTOMOBILE DEALERSHIP, LLC, D.B.A, TORIAN HOLDINGS, and NATASHA AZZAM's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Debra Lynn Pulliam, To Submit To Mental Examination.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT, CONCORD AUTOMOBILE DEALERSHIP LLC HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OF "CAD") HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR REQUIRING PLAINTIFF, DEBRA LYNN PULLIAM (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR "PULLLIAM") TO SUBMIT TO A MENTAL EXAMINATION ("IME) PURSUANT TO THE PROVISIONS OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2032.310, ET SEQ. HOWEVER THE COURT IS NOT SATISFIED WITH THE SCOPE AND SPECIFICITY OF THE MENTAL EXAMINATION SET FORTH IN DEFENDANT'S DEMAND FOR EXAMINATION SERVED ON PLAINTIFF ON JANUARY 4, 2023. (SEE VINSON v. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 43 CAL 3d 837, 839, ET SEQ.) ACCORDINGLY, IF DEFENDANT STILL WISHES TO PROCEED WITH IT'S DEMAND FOR THE IME, THE DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PROMPTLY MEET AND CONFER WITH COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IN THE HOPE THAT THEY CAN ARRIVE AT THE CONTENTS OF A STIPULATED DEMAND NOW THAT THE COURT HAS RULED AS TO DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO AN IME. AT A MINIMUM THE DEMAND SHOULD INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING TERMS:
(1) THE TIME, PLACE AND WHETHER IT MAY BE CONDUCTED REMOTELY.
(2) THE TOTAL TIME FOR THE IME, INCLUDING THE EXAMINATION AND TESTING, WILL NOT EXCEED SEVEN HOURS, EXCLUDING BREAKS FOR MEALS OR REST
(3) THE IDENTITY OF THE. INDIVIDUAL CONDUCTING THE EXAMINATION AS WELL AS THE INDIVIDUAL CONDUCTING THE TESTING.
(4) DEFINE WITH SPECIFICITY THE TESTS TO BE ADMINISTERED AND HOW THEY RELATE TO PLAINTIFF'S PRESENT CONDITION.
(5) A DESCRIPTION OF THE SCOPE OF THE EXAMINATION AND AN EXPLANATION AS TO HOW THE AREAS TO BE COVERED RELATE TO PLAINTIFF'S PRESENT CONDITION.
(6) IF AFTER SEVEN (7) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER THE PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO ARRIVE AS A STIPULATED DEMAND, DEFENDANT SHOULD DELIVER TO THE COURT ITS DEMAND REJECTED BY PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF SHOULD DELIVER TO THE COURT A SHORT MEMORANDUM (NO MORE THAN TEN PAGES) SETTING FORTH ITS OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND.
(7) FOLLOWING RECEIPT OF THE ABOVE DOCUMENTS THE COURT WILL ADVISE THE PARTIES OF ITS DECISION AND REQUEST THAT THE PREVAILING PARTY PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE ULTIMATE DETERMINATION.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DEBRA PULLIAM'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a First Amended Complaint is denied without prejudice. The motion was not timely filed and served in compliance with CCP 1005(b). The motion also lacks a points and authorities in compliance with California Rule of Court ("CRC") 3.1113(a). Lastly, the motion does not comply with the mandates of CRC 3.1324 to bring a motion to amend. Plaintiff fails to differentiate between the original and proposed first amended complaint and she does not provide a declaration in compliance with that rule. Plaintiff may attempt to fix these defects and re-new the motion.

Court to review submittted proposed order.
